name: A Jellie of Fyshe
recipeIngredient: 1 oz Ginger root, peeled and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the codfish (or other white fish) in a pan with the onions, vinegar, ginger root, spices, wine, and water. Gently bring to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
2. Add the scallops and prawns and cook for an additional 3 minutes.
3. Remove the fish; bone and skin the white fish and set it aside.
4. Strain the cooking juices and let cool for several hours, allowing sediment to settle at the bottom of the bowl.
5. Carefully pour off the juices, leaving the sediment, and strain through a clean teacloth multiple times. You should have about 3 cups of liquid remaining.
6. Melt 3/4 oz gelatin in a small amount of the cooled liquid, then mix it into the rest of the juices.
7. Pour a thin layer (1/2 inch) of the juice into the bottom of a 5-cup souffle dish or fish mold and refrigerate to set.
8. Flake the white fish into small pieces; remove coral from scallops and cut into three or four pieces.
9. Once the jelly is firm, arrange the most decorative elements of the fish in the bottom of the dish—scallops in the center, prawns around the edges, and flakes of white fish between them or as desired.
10. Spoon a little more juice back into the mold and return it to the refrigerator to set.
11. Continue layering the fish in the mold, covering each layer with juice until all fish and juices are used up.
12. Leave the jelly to set for at least 4 hours in the refrigerator before unmolding.
13. Decorate with fresh herbs and serve as a starter.
